ops.title: ccx exec history — the gomonty dylib/notarization era (v0.4.0–v0.5.1) ended in v0.6.0; the sandbox is now a per-run uv subprocess running pydantic-monty, no dylib, no entitlement, Intel Macs supported

ops.body:
`ccx exec` (internal/codeexec) runs a monty-subset Python script whose async host functions are every ccx op + gated `sh()` + every stateless MCP server's tools (auto-reflected from `claude mcp list`). Only the return value enters context.

**Current state (v0.6.0, 2026-07-07):** the sandbox is a per-run subprocess — `uv run --no-project --no-config --no-build --quiet --python 3.13 --with pydantic-monty==0.0.18 python <cached driver.py>` — speaking JSON Lines over stdin/stdout. The embedded gomonty dylib, the `disable-library-validation` entitlement, macOS notarization, the Intel-mac stub, and the `GOMONTY_FFI_CACHE_DIR` shim are all GONE; binaries are back to ~11 MB and darwin/amd64 runs exec. `internal/codeexec/driver.py` is the only code touching the pydantic_monty API (the upstream pool-architecture release becomes a driver-only upgrade). The driver cache copy is byte-verified against the embedded source before every exec (never trust the filename). The pin lives in `driver.go` (`montyRequirement`) — bump only in dedicated PRs that re-run the codeexec suite.

**Historical gotcha (kept for context):** v0.4.0–v0.5.1 embedded gomonty's ad-hoc-signed dylib; notarized darwin binaries rejected it at Dlopen without the entitlement, invisible in local unsigned builds — that's why a post-release smoke job runs `ccx exec '40+2'` against the released tarball. The smoke job survives v0.6.0 repurposed: it now validates the uv-provisioned driver chain an end user hits.

**How to apply:** `claude mcp list` latency still floors CLI `ccx exec` (~4.5s; the disk catalog caches schemas, not the probe). `CCX_EXEC_MCP=off` / `_DENY` / `_ALLOW` override reflection. The warm per-run uv spawn is ~0.1s; the pydantic-monty wheel is per-CPython-ABI, hence the pinned `--python 3.13`.

Related: [[cc-context-architecture]], [[gomonty-partial-drain-double-dispatch]].
